data.content:
I/O System
The I/O System encompasses all hardware and logic responsible for input and output operations in a computer system.

Data Port
A Data Port is a type of I/O port specifically used for the transmission of data between the computer and I/O devices.

Block Device
A Block Device is an I/O device that manages data in fixed-size blocks, allowing random access and storage operations.

Medium-Speed Device
A Medium-Speed Device is an I/O device category characterized by moderate data transfer rates.

Serial Interface
The Serial Interface is an I/O interface that transmits data sequentially, one bit at a time, over a single channel.

Input/Output Device
An Input/Output Device supports both input and output functionalities, enabling two-way data exchange with the computer.

I/O Device
I/O Devices are peripheral hardware components that facilitate input, output, or both between the computer and the external environment.

I/O Channel
The I/O Channel is a dedicated data path or protocol that manages communication between the CPU, memory, and I/O devices.

Low-Speed Device
A Low-Speed Device is a classification of I/O devices that operate at a relatively slow data transfer rate.

I/O Port
I/O Port is a specific terminal or access point through which data and control signals are sent to or received from an I/O device.

Parallel Interface
The Parallel Interface is a type of I/O interface where multiple data bits are transmitted simultaneously over several channels.

Input Device
An Input Device is a category of I/O devices designed to capture data from the external world and transmit it to the computer.

Output Device
An Output Device is a type of I/O device that presents or sends processed data from the computer to the external environment.

High-Speed Device
A High-Speed Device is a type of I/O device that supports fast data transfer rates.

I/O Interface
The I/O Interface is a hardware and protocol mechanism that connects I/O devices to the computer system, managing communication and data exchange.

Character Device
A Character Device is an I/O device that manages data as a stream of characters, typically handling data sequentially.

Status Port
A Status Port is an I/O port designed for communicating the current status or condition of an I/O device to the computer system.

Control Logic
Control Logic refers to the circuitry or mechanism within the I/O controller that manages device control operations.

Control Port
Control port is a type of I/O port used for sending commands or control signals to I/O devices.

Select Channel
Select Channel is a type of I/O channel optimized for managing communications with a single device at a time.

Host Interface
The Host Interface is a component within the I/O controller that connects and coordinates communication with the main computer.

Device Interface
Device interface is a component of the I/O controller used for communication with the I/O device.

Programmed I/O
Programmed I/O is a data transfer method where the CPU actively checks I/O device status and handles all data transmissions.<SEP>Programmed I/O is a method where the CPU is directly responsible for data transfer to and from I/O devices, requiring constant software polling.

Multiplex Channel
Multiplex Channel is a type of I/O channel capable of managing communication with multiple devices simultaneously.

Interrupt-Driven I/O
Interrupt-Driven I/O is a transfer method where I/O devices notify the CPU via interrupts when ready, prompting the CPU to perform data transfer only upon request.<SEP>Interrupt-Driven I/O is a data transfer method where I/O devices signal the CPU via interrupts when ready for communication, reducing CPU involvement.

DMA
DMA (Direct Memory Access) is a method that allows a controller to directly manage data transfers between memory and I/O devices, minimizing CPU involvement.<SEP>DMA (Direct Memory Access) is an I/O method where data can be transferred between memory and I/O devices independently of the CPU, managed by a dedicated controller.

Status Detection
Status detection is a function of I/O interfaces that monitors the operational state of I/O devices.

Data Format Conversion
Data format conversion is a function of the I/O interface that translates data between different formats required by devices and the host system.

DMA Controller
DMA Controller is the hardware component responsible for conducting direct memory access transfers between system memory and I/O devices.

Data Path
Data path is a component of I/O interface responsible for the transmission of data signals.

Address Recognition
Address recognition is a feature of I/O interfaces used to identify and select the correct hardware address for data transfers.

Status/Control Path
Status/control path is a component of I/O interface used to transmit status and control signals between the CPU and I/O devices.

Address Path
Address path is a part of the I/O interface that carries address signals for identifying device or memory locations.

Selector Channel
Selector channel is a type of I/O channel dedicated to handling data transfers for a single device at a time.

Buffered Operation
Buffered operation refers to the process in I/O interfaces that temporarily stores data to accommodate differences in speed between I/O devices and other system components.

I/O Controller
The I/O Controller is a component that manages and coordinates data exchange between the computer and its I/O devices.

Multiplexer Channel
Multiplexer channel is a type of I/O channel that can control multiple devices concurrently by interleaving their data transfers.
The image is a detailed mind map diagram titled 'Chap7 I/O系统' (Chapter 7 I/O System), visually organ

I/O System Basic Concepts
I/O System Basic Concepts covers the foundational ideas and general principles underlying input/output systems within the context of computer architecture.

Controller Structure
Controller Structure entails the design and architecture of I/O controllers, including their internal components and logical organization.

I/O Ports
I/O Ports are hardware access points for connecting I/O devices to a computer, described in various types and technical details within the mind map.

Interface Functions
Interface Functions describe the specific roles and operations performed by I/O interfaces in facilitating communication between devices and the computer system.

I/O Channels
I/O Channels represent dedicated communication pathways or subsystems used for data transfer between the CPU and I/O devices.

I/O Devices
I/O Devices are components of a computer system responsible for input and output operations, categorized and discussed as part of the mind map structure.

Port Types
Port Types represent the various forms or standards of I/O ports considered in the mind map, outlining technical distinctions between implementations.

Chap7 I/O System
Chap7 I/O System is a mind map diagram visually organizing the major concepts and structural elements of the I/O system as described in Chapter 7 of a computer architecture context.

I/O Interfaces
I/O Interfaces refer to the mechanisms and connective elements that allow communication between I/O devices and the rest of the computer system, including topics such as interface functions and types.

I/O Methods
I/O Methods include the techniques for implementing data transfer between the computer and I/O devices, such as programmed I/O, interrupt-driven I/O, and direct memory access (DMA).

Device Classification
Device Classification refers to the categorization of I/O devices into different types based on their characteristics or usage, depicted as a subtopic in the mind map.

Data Transfer Methods
Data Transfer Methods refers to specific approaches used to move data between computers and I/O devices, exemplified by programmed I/O, interrupt-driven I/O, and DMA.

I/O Controllers
I/O Controllers are dedicated hardware components that manage the operation of I/O devices, including structure and internal logic as illustrated in the diagram.

李飞飞
李飞飞is an individual who graduated from Princeton University in 1999, studied Tibetan medicine in Tibet for a year, earned a PhD from California Institute of Technology in 2005, and held assistant professor positions at University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and Princeton University.

跨学科背景
跨学科背景, or interdisciplinary background, refers to the diverse academic and research experiences acquired by李飞飞through her educational and professional path.

加州理工学院
加州理工学院is a research university where李飞飞was a graduate student from 2000 and received a PhD in 2005.

高等教育经历
高等教育经历refers to李飞飞's progression through multiple stages of higher education at prestigious institutions.

Tibet
Tibet is the region where Li Feifei conducted research on traditional Tibetan medicine for one year after graduating from Princeton University.

学术职位变迁
学术职位变迁describes the changes in academic positions held by李飞飞throughout her career at several top universities.

data.entity_name: 伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校
data.content:
伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校
伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校is a university where李飞飞served as an assistant professor in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering from 2005 to 2006.

西藏
西藏is a region where李飞飞spent a year conducting research on Tibetan medicine after her undergraduate graduation.

普林斯顿大学
普林斯顿大学is a higher education institution from which李飞飞obtained her undergraduate degree and later served as an assistant professor in the Department of Computer Science.

Traditional Tibetan Medicine
Traditional Tibetan Medicine is the medicinal practice and research topic that Li Feifei studied during her year in Tibet.

藏药
藏药refers to Tibetan medicine, a traditional system that was the subject of a year-long research project by李飞飞in Tibet after her undergraduate studies.

Li Feifei
Li Feifei traveled to Tibet to conduct research on traditional Tibetan medicine for one year after her undergraduate graduation.
